problem.title: Reorder List
problem.description: You are given the head of a singly linked-list. The list can be represented as: L0 → L1 → … → Ln - 1 → Ln. Reorder the list to be on the following form: L0 → Ln → L1 → Ln - 1 → L2 → Ln - 2 → …
assumptions: Do not return anything; modify head in-place.

example.explanation: Interleave: L0→Ln→L1→Ln-1→... Find mid, reverse second half, merge.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Find Middle, Reverse, Merge
solutions.description: Split the list at its midpoint using slow/fast pointers, reverse the second half, then interleave the two halves by alternating nodes from each. This in-place approach avoids any auxiliary data structures and runs in O(n) time.
solutions.code_python:
from typing import Optional

# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
#         self.val = val
#         self.next = next

def reorderList(head: Optional[object]) -> None:
    if not head or not head.next:
        return

    # Step 1: find the middle
    slow, fast = head, head
    while fast.next and fast.next.next:
        slow = slow.next
        fast = fast.next.next

    # Step 2: reverse the second half
    second = slow.next
    slow.next = None
    prev = None
    while second:
        nxt = second.next
        second.next = prev
        prev = second
        second = nxt
    second = prev  # head of reversed second half

    # Step 3: interleave the two halves
    first = head
    while second:
        tmp1, tmp2 = first.next, second.next
        first.next = second
        second.next = tmp1
        first = tmp1
        second = tmp2
